headline: MUSTANG ENERGY HITS 9.39% U3O8 IN SURFACE SAMPLES AT NEW ATHABASCA BASIN URANIUM PROJECT
snippet: Mustang Energy Corp. reported grab samples returning up to 9.39% U3O8 at its Surprise Creek Uranium-Copper Project in Saskatchewan, northwest of the Athabasca Basin. Uranium mineralization traced over ~500m of strike, with copper up to 3.11% Cu at adjacent Bob Lake and Maimann Lake targets.

headline: USA RARE EARTH COMMISSIONS FIRST U.S. SINTERED MAGNET PRODUCTION LINE; USAR STOCK +7%
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) commissioned Phase 1a of its Stillwater, Oklahoma NdFeB sintered magnet line on April 17 — the first domestic rare earth magnet production in the US. The company is backed by $3.1B in combined government (CHIPS Act) and private capital, targeting 1,200 t/yr of magnet capacity by early 2027.

headline: COSA RESOURCES HITS 13,900 CPS OVER 5M AT MURPHY LAKE NORTH URANIUM — DENISON JV, ATHABASCA BASIN
snippet: Cosa Resources (TSX-V: COSA, 70% JV) and Denison Mines (30%) intersected 5.0m of anomalous radioactivity up to 13,900 CPS in the upper basement at Murphy Lake North — 3km east of IsoEnergy's Hurricane Deposit in the eastern Athabasca Basin. Mineralization open for at least 600m in both directions; chemical assays pending.

headline: FOREMOST CLEAN ENERGY DRILLS 1.0% U3O8 AT HATCHET LAKE SOUTH; TUNING FORK ZONE NOW 150M STRIKE
snippet: Foremost Clean Energy (NASDAQ: FMST) intersected 1.4m of 1.0% eU3O8 within 4.6m of 0.34% eU3O8 at the Athabasca unconformity at Hatchet Lake South, Saskatchewan. Five of ten drill holes confirmed mineralization; the Tuning Fork Uranium Zone now extends over 150m of strike, up from a single discovery hole.

headline: ABRASILVER HITS 171 METRES AT 0.83 G/T GOLD AT DIABLILLOS — OCULTO EAST TARGET KEEPS EXPANDING
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. (TSX: ABRA) reported Phase VI drill results from its Diablillos project in Argentina: hole DDH 26-008 returned 171m grading 0.83 g/t gold and 10.3 g/t silver from 230m depth, including 9m at 3.46 g/t gold. Results continue to expand the open-pit resource beyond the current Mineral Resource estimate at Oculto East.

headline: K92 MINING POSTS RECORD LATERAL DEVELOPMENT IN Q1 2026 AS KAINANTU STAGE 3 RAMP-UP ADVANCES
snippet: K92 Mining (TSX: KNT) produced 46,743 oz AuEq (44,022 oz gold) in Q1 2026 from its Kainantu Mine in Papua New Guinea, in line with budget. Record quarterly mine development of 3,007m — up 21% from Q1 2025 — with a new March monthly record of 1,067m signals headroom ahead of Stage 3 expansion. Annual guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq maintained.

headline: GOLD SWOT: CHINA IMPORTING 790+ TONNES OF SILVER IN EARLY 2026 — SOLAR DEMAND RESHAPING THE MARKET
snippet: This week's Gold SWOT analysis from US Global Investors flags China importing over 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026 as the standout bullish signal, driven largely by solar panel manufacturing. The gold-to-silver ratio contracting from above 105 is identified as a historically reliable precursor to silver outperformance.

headline: G MINING BUYS G2 GOLDFIELDS FOR $2.2B IN GUYANA PLAY — 72% PREMIUM SIGNALS HOT JUNIOR M&A MARKET
snippet: G Mining Ventures (TSX: GMIN) agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-share deal valued at ~$2.2B (C$3B), offering a 72% premium to G2 shareholders based on 30-day VWAPs as of April 8. The combined Guyana assets — Oko West and Oko-Ghanie — target 500,000+ oz/yr gold production, unlocking C$1B+ in infrastructure synergies.

headline: CHINA BANS SULFURIC ACID EXPORTS FROM MAY — SILVER MINERS COULD BENEFIT AS SUPPLY CHAIN STRESS MOUNTS
snippet: China will halt sulfuric acid exports beginning in May 2026, citing Iran war-driven supply shocks. Since ~70% of newly mined silver is produced as a byproduct of copper and lead-zinc smelting — which requires sulfuric acid for leaching — analysts warn the ban could worsen silver's already projected sixth consecutive annual supply deficit.

headline: CENTRAL BANK GOLD BUYING SLOWS — BUT METALS FOCUS SAYS TREND REVERSAL IS TEMPORARY
snippet: Central bank gold purchases slowed sharply in recent months, prompting concern among gold bulls. Metals Focus analysts argue the pullback is a temporary reaction to high spot prices — not a structural shift — and that official-sector demand remains on track to reach up to 900 tonnes for 2026, with the People's Bank of China still actively accumulating.
